project: NAFTA Gbely — Trebišov Gas Field Documentation
description: Field documentation of decommissioned natural gas wellheads operated by NAFTA a.s. Gbely / NAFTA VÝCHOD a.s. in Trebišov, eastern Slovakia. Five wells Trebišov 8–12, Christmas tree assemblies, both blue (Soviet-era gate valve) and yellow (1996 ball valve) designs.

wells.operator: NAFTA VÝCHOD a.s., Michalovce (subsidiary of NAFTA a.s. Gbely)
wells.collection_station: Zberné plynové stredisko (Gas Collection Station), Trebišov

blue_tree.distinguishing_features: Boxy square actuator housings, two casing outlet assemblies at base with individual gauges indicating at least 3 concentric casings

yellow_tree.actuators: Pneumatic actuator disc on left wing arm — remote/auto operation
yellow_tree.distinguishing_features: Spherical ball valve bodies, large multi-bolt pneumatic actuator disc on left wing, more compact profile

geothermal_conversion.purpose: District heating network for city of Trebišov
geothermal_conversion.significance: First project of this kind in Slovakia

geology.basin: East Slovak Basin — Neogene back-arc basin (part of Pannonian Basin system)
geology.reservoir_age: Badenian and Lower Sarmatian (~15 million years old)
geology.tectonic_origin: Back-arc extension following Outer Carpathian subduction

nafta_company.founded: Origins in 1913 Gbely oil discovery; industrial production from 13 January 1914
nafta_company.eastern_subsidiary: NAFTA VÝCHOD a.s., Michalovce — created 1996 during restructuring
nafta_company.exploration_partner: Aspect Holdings (via Slovakian Horizon Energy s.r.o.)

privatisation_1996.opposition_phrase: theft of the century / lúpež storočia
privatisation_1996.aftermath: Criminal charges filed 1998; 40.9% of shares returned to state FNM March 1999

notes: Wikipedia URL for Trebišov was previously misspelled as Trabisov — correct URL uses Trebi%C5%A1ov
Exploration partner is Aspect Holdings (not Aspect Energy as earlier stated)
Pavlovce nad Uhom area is 1,070 km² (not round 1,000 km²)
Ownership percentages (SPP 56.15%, Czech Gas Holding 40.45%) are on the news page, not the about-us page
Two Slovak Spectator articles are 403 to bots but fully accessible in a browser
Production started 1996 (not the 1960s — that was when eastern Slovak gas production generally began under Czechoslovakia)
